title: Slope Calculator – Two Points to Slope & Angle
shortDescription: Find the slope, angle, and grade from two points on a line.
description: Enter two points (x₁,y₁) and (x₂,y₂) to get the slope, the angle in degrees, and the grade as a percentage. Runs in your browser — no signup.
metaDescription: Free slope calculator. Enter two points to find the slope of a line, the angle in degrees, and the grade percentage. Shows the rise-over-run formula instantly.

result.empty: Enter two points to calculate the slope.

sections.heading: How slope is calculated
paragraphs: Slope is the steepness of a line, found by dividing the change in y by the change in x between two points: m = (y₂ − y₁) ÷ (x₂ − x₁), often called rise over run. A positive slope rises left to right, a negative slope falls, and a horizontal line has slope zero.

sections.heading: Angle and grade
paragraphs: The angle of the line is arctangent of the slope, in degrees. The grade is the slope expressed as a percentage (slope × 100), commonly used for roads and ramps. A vertical line (x₂ = x₁) has an undefined slope because the run is zero.

howTo.name: Read the slope
howTo.text: See the slope, angle in degrees, and grade percentage.

faq.a: m = (y₂ − y₁) ÷ (x₂ − x₁), the change in y divided by the change in x.

faq.q: What does a negative slope mean?
faq.a: The line goes down from left to right; y decreases as x increases.
A vertical line has x₂ = x₁, so the run is zero and division is undefined.

faq.q: How do I convert slope to an angle?
faq.a: Take the arctangent of the slope and convert to degrees: angle = atan(m) × 180/π.
Grade is the slope as a percentage (slope × 100), used for road and ramp inclines.
